paper_title: Blow-ups and extensions of trees in tournaments
summary: Conjecture 9 from arXiv:2410.23566 asserts that there exists a constant C such that unvd(D) ≤ C·unvd(D−v) for every acyclic digraph D and every vertex v, where unvd(D) is the smallest order of a tournament that contains D. The paper itself proves the special case C=2 when v is a source or sink (Proposition 1), but the general case remains open. No follow-up paper resolving the conjecture was found in a targeted web search covering the period 2024–2026.
search_queries: Aboulker Havet Picasarri-Arrieta "unvd" acyclic digraph tournament blow-ups trees 2024
Aboulker Havet Lopes "extensions of trees in tournaments" "acyclic digraph" vertex deletion 2025
notes: The function unvd(D) is the smallest integer p such that D is contained in every tournament of order p. Conjecture 9 generalises Proposition 1 (source/sink case, C=2) to arbitrary vertices. The conjecture is intimately linked to Sumner's conjecture (Conjecture 4) and to Conjecture 10 on k-extensions of oriented trees: together, Conjectures 9 and 4 would imply Conjecture 10. The paper arXiv:2603.13955 found in the search concerns digraph subdivisions of C(k,ℓ) cycles and is unrelated. No follow-up was found; open with high confidence given the conjecture is recent (Oct 2024).
